question.text: What common theme emerges from the PMLA enforcement cases studied in the NISM IFSCA-01 curriculum?

answer.text: Reporting entities face significant penalties for failing to implement effective AML/CFT systems including KYC verification, ongoing due diligence, record maintenance, and suspicious transaction reporting
PMLA enforcement actions are limited to warnings and no monetary penalties are ever imposed
Only large multinational banks are subject to PMLA enforcement, while smaller entities are exempt
PMLA enforcement cases are purely theoretical and have never resulted in actual penalties

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Across all cases discussed — Bybit (Rs 9.27 crore penalty), Paytm (multiple charges), Prowess (authorization cancelled), Way2Wealth (compliance deficiencies) — the common theme is that reporting entities face real, significant consequences for failing to implement effective AML/CFT systems covering KYC, ongoing CDD, record-keeping, and STR filing. **The Trap:** Option A is flatly incorrect — substantial monetary penalties and even cancellation of authorizations have been imposed. Option B wrongly suggests size-based exemption — entities of all sizes face enforcement. **Cross-Exam Context:** These case studies are tested in NISM IFSCA-01 to reinforce that AML compliance is not optional and has real consequences. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Invest proactively in robust AML/CFT infrastructure — the cost of compliance is always lower than the cost of enforcement actions, reputational damage, and business disruption.
